topology.title: Network Topology

topology.ghost_info_desc: If successful, the device is added to inventory and LLDP neighbors are discovered. Otherwise, you will be asked to enter credentials.
topology.auto_login_desc: Enter username and password to connect to {{ip}}.
topology.cred_tried: {{count}} credentials tried, login failed. Please enter your credentials.
topology.hop_suggestion: You can expand the map by sending LLDP to these switches.
topology.blast_critical: CRITICAL — {{count}} devices will lose connectivity if this device goes offline
topology.blast_safe: Network is resilient — sub-networks will remain connected if this device goes offline

agents.title: Proxy Agents
agents.create_desc: Once the agent is created, you can download a personalized installer script. Run it once — it registers as a service and starts automatically after reboot.
agents.delete_desc: Devices assigned to this agent may be affected.
agents.created_warning: The agent key is shown only once. Save it now!
agents.linux_hint: Paste the command into a terminal, or download the .sh file and run with sudo bash. Root access required.
agents.windows_hint: Paste the command into an elevated PowerShell window, or download the .ps1 file and run as administrator.
agents.server_url_label: Server Address (URL the agent will connect to)
agents.server_url_hint: If the agent runs on a different machine, enter the real IP or hostname. e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8000
agents.no_agents_desc: The agent is installed on your local network and handles all SSH communication. Install it once on an administrator's PC or server — it starts automatically on every reboot.

help.title: Help & Getting Started
help.subtitle: Everything about using Charon and its features
help.qs_step1_desc: Go to Devices page and click + Add Device. Enter the IP address, SSH credentials and vendor type.
help.qs_step2_desc: For access from different network segments, go to Agents page and install a proxy agent.
help.qs_step3_desc: In the Topology page, click 'LLDP Discover'. Neighboring switches are discovered automatically.
help.qs_step4_desc: Create automatic alert rules from Alert Rules page and track events from Monitor.
help.feat_dashboard_desc: Overall network health score, online/offline device counts, recent events and quick actions.
help.feat_devices_desc: SSH connectivity, port status, bulk credential updates and configuration management.
help.feat_topology_desc: Automatic network map via LLDP, layer filtering, ghost switch discovery and blast radius analysis.
help.feat_twin_desc: Takes a snapshot (baseline) of your network topology and automatically compares every change. 'New Connection' means an unauthorized cable or device; 'Lost Connection' means a disconnected link. If changes were planned, click 'New Baseline' to accept the current state. Access via Topology → Digital Twin.
help.feat_monitor_desc: Real-time alerts for device offline, CPU/memory threshold breaches and other critical events.
help.feat_backups_desc: Automatic or manual device config backups with version diff analysis.
help.feat_ipam_desc: IP address management, subnet planning and utilization monitoring.
help.feat_vlan_desc: Create, delete and apply VLANs to switches.
help.feat_compliance_desc: Automatically verify that configurations meet security and standards compliance rules.
help.feat_bandwidth_desc: Per-port traffic monitoring via SNMP with ingress/egress graphs and utilization analytics.
help.feat_security_desc: Detect security vulnerabilities, weak passwords and misconfigurations in your network.
help.feat_playbooks_desc: Automate repetitive network tasks. Supports scheduled or event-based triggering.
help.feat_change_desc: Plan, approve and execute network changes with a full audit trail.
help.feat_sla_desc: Device and service uptime tracking, SLA compliance reports and trend analysis.
help.feat_agents_desc: Distributed agent infrastructure for SSH access across different network segments.
help.feat_racks_desc: Visually manage physical server rack and hardware placements.
help.feat_floor_desc: Position and visualize devices on building floor plans.
help.feat_diagnostics_desc: Real-time network diagnostics with ping, traceroute and port scanning tools.
help.feat_reports_desc: Inventory, event history and backup status reports with CSV export.
help.feat_snmptrap_desc: Real-time linkDown/linkUp event capture from devices via agent UDP 1620.
help.feat_maintenance_desc: Suppress alerts during scheduled downtime; one-shot or recurring (daily/weekly/monthly).
help.feat_terminal_desc: Browser-based SSH terminal via agent relay; dangerous commands gated, all keystrokes audited.
help.feat_syslog_desc: Agent collects syslog on UDP 514; facility/severity filtering, per-device view.
help.feat_poe_desc: Side menu → PoE / Energy. Per-device live PoE power draw (Watts). Background SNMP snapshot every 15 minutes via POWER-ETHERNET-MIB; for real mW open the device drawer and click 'Live SSH (Real W)' — runs 'show poe interfaces status' (Ruijie) or 'show power inline' (Cisco). Each port shows PD class (1-8), state, power, last update. 'Fetch (Live)' button parallel-SNMP probes the entire fleet.
help.feat_firmware_desc: Side menu → Firmware (org_admin+). Two tabs: Catalog + Install Jobs. Add firmware via file upload (SHA256 streamed) or URL catalog (TFTP/HTTP/SCP). Install worker takes a PRE-RESTORE snapshot, transfers + boot-set + save, then pauses at 'awaiting_reload'. Operator approves the reboot from the drawer; post-version is verified after the device comes back.
help.feat_configbuilder_desc: Side menu → Easy Config Builder. Add VLAN / tag a port / set NTP-syslog-SNMP-DNS without writing CLI. Vendor-aware command generation with live CLI box. Preview with vendor/OS only — no device needed; download as .txt. Batch: add multiple operations to the cart, apply all at once. 9 operations × 5 vendor families (Cisco/Aruba/Ruijie/HP/Comware).
help.feat_configrollback_desc: Backup Center → expand a device → click RollbackOutlined next to a backup. Side-by-side 'active vs target' cards + live diff preview. On confirm, a PRE-RESTORE snapshot is taken first (safety), then the old config is pushed and write memory runs. Skipped if device is already on the target hash.
help.feat_bulkops_desc: Devices page: select rows, then toolbar offers Tag · Lifecycle (production/passive/stock/archived) · Move Location · Assign Agent · Update Password · Bulk Backup · Fetch Info · Delete. Each bulk action is audit-logged; failed devices appear in a 'skipped' list with reasons.
help.feat_sshaudit_desc: Side menu → SSH Session Audit. Records every interactive SSH session opened from the browser — user, device, start/end, extracted commands (backspace/ESC sequences cleaned), IO byte counts, last ~10KB output excerpt, exit reason. Idle sessions over 30 min auto-close as 'stale_cleanup'.
help.feat_aisummary_desc: SSH Session Audit drawer → 'Generate AI Summary' button. Sends the command list + output excerpt to your AI provider (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini / Ollama — picked in Settings) and returns a 2-3 sentence summary of what the operator did. Forensic audit + reporting.
help.feat_porttoggle_desc: Devices → ApiOutlined icon on a device card → /devices/{id}/ports. Per-port On/Off + PoE On/Off buttons. Click opens a 'reason' textarea modal with 5-min auto-rollback timer. Confirm within the timer = permanent; otherwise the system auto-runs the inverse command (safety pattern: prevents accidental self-lockout).
help.feat_cyclicmw_desc: Settings → Maintenance Windows → New Window → 'Recurrence' section: Daily/Weekly/Monthly. For weekly pick the days, for monthly pick day-of-month (1-28). An hourly beat task materializes the next 14 days of instances; each child instance behaves as a normal one-shot window for alert suppression.
help.feat_systemset_desc: Settings → System Settings tab (super_admin). Scan sensitivity (timeout/retry/threshold), maintenance-during-scan behavior, agent revalidate interval, snapshot retention, etc. All settings are JSON-typed with cache + audit trail.
help.feat_pwdpolicy_desc: Settings → Password Policy tab (org_admin). Min length, complexity (upper/lower/digit/symbol), history reuse ban (last N), forced rotation in days. Login response carries password_expired flag and forces the change screen.
help.feat_mfaemail_desc: Profile → Multi-Factor Authentication tab. After enrolling TOTP (Google Authenticator etc.) you can also enable Email OTP. Login offers method picker; 6-digit OTP valid 10 minutes, bcrypt-hashed in DB, single-use.
help.feat_ipallowlist_desc: Profile → IP Allowlist tab. Restrict your account to specific IPs / CIDRs. CSV: '10.0.0.0/8, 192.168.1.5'. 'Add My Current IP' button for quick fill. Self-lockout protection: if your current request IP isn't in the new list, save is rejected with 409. Empty = no restriction.
help.feat_ipamtools_desc: IPAM → Subnet drawer: 'Fill from ARP' (pulls device ARP caches, adds matching IPs as assignments, auto-fills hostnames from your Device table) and 'IP Scan (Ping)' (parallel ICMP sweep, adds responding IPs as 'discovery' source, removes the ones that no longer respond — manual entries untouched). Quick way to populate a fresh subnet.
help.role_super_admin_desc: Full access to all permissions and all organizations.
help.role_admin_desc: Full operations within own organization including user management.
help.role_operator_desc: Device, task and configuration management. Cannot create users.
help.role_viewer_desc: Read-only access. Cannot make any changes.
